title: 23.-Contract No. 081671-1 (8318C) Amendment: RouteSmart Technologies, Inc. Software Maintenance

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 81671-1
(8318C) with RouteSmart Technologies Inc. to increase the contract by $25,000, for a
total not to exceed amount of $235,743, to fund software license maintenance and
support services, from June 30, 2025 through June 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funds for this contract are available in FY 2025 and subject to appropriation in FY 2026
in the Zero Waste Fund 601 (601-54-627-734-3023-000-472-613130).
sections.current_situation:
The Department of Public Works uses RouteSmart to manage over 27,000 refuse, 20,000
green waste, and 5,000 commercial recycling stops each month. Prior to RouteSmart
implementation, staff used the FUND$ Utility Billing (CX) module, originally designed as
a water utility billing system. Because that module was not designed to manage refuse
billing and account management, routes were improperly managed. Poorly managed
routes led to unbalanced workload distribution, slow route completion times, and
community complaints. Since implementation in 2010, RouteSmart has helped staff
optimize the workload of 16 crews running a total of nearly 80 routes per week.
sections.background:
The City issued a Request for Proposals (Specification N. 09-10440-C) for route
optimization software in July 2009, and received three responses. The RFP responses
were evaluated by staff from the Public Works, Information Technology and Finance
Departments; and RouteSmart Technologies Inc. was identified as the vendor that could
provide the best value to the City. In 2010, RouteSmart Technologies Inc. successfully
installed its software, configured integration with the FUND$ Utility Billing (CX) module,
and provided training to Zero Waste Division staff.

Internal
Contract No. 8318C Amendment: RouteSmart Technologies, Inc. Software Maintenance CONSENT CALENDAR
May 20, 2025
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
RouteSmart software identifies the shortest, most efficient route possible for Zero Waste
Division trucks, which helps reduce the City’s carbon footprint.
sections.rationale:
RouteSmart software has improved pickup times, balanced workloads, and enhanced
customer service. RouteSmart Technologies, Inc. has delivered consistent, high quality
support and consulting services .
sections.alternatives:
Zero Waste considered hiring a consultant to maintain the RouteSmart system.
However, employing a consultant would be more costly than retaining RouteSmart
Technologies Inc. to provide ongoing maintenance and support. If ongoing maintenance
is discontinued, staff would pursue an alternative system and invest resources into
ensuring integration with existing City financial systems.
CONTACT PERSON
Leticia Jaurequi, Solid Waste & Recycling Manager, Public Works, (510) 981-6362

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT AMENDMENT: CONTRACT NO. 81671-1 (8318C) – ROUTESMART
TECHNOLOGY INC. FOR ZERO WASTE ROUTE OPTIMIZATION SOFTWARE
MAINTENANCE
WHEREAS, prior to the RouteSmart Technologies software implementation,
maintenance of Zero Waste (previously Solid Waste) routes was conducted via a very
time-consuming, manual process; and
WHEREAS, in 2009, the City issued a Request for Proposal (Specification No. 09-
10440-C) for route optimization software, in an effort make the routing process more
efficient; and
WHEREAS, a review panel including staff in the Zero Waste Division, Information
Technology Department, and Finance Department performed a thorough evaluation of
the proposals and determined that RouteSmart Technologies could best meet the City’s
needs; and
WHEREAS, the RouteSmart Technologies software has greatly reduced staff time
required to maintain the routes, increased the efficiency of the pickup schedules, and
led to improved customer satisfaction; and
WHEREAS, City and Contractor previously entered into Contract Number 8318, dated
April 28, 2010 which Contract was authorized by the Berkeley City Council by
Resolution No. 64,667 – N.S; and
WHEREAS, on April 9, 2014, the City Manager authorized an amendment to extend the
contract through June 30, 2014; and
WHEREAS, on June 3, 2014, by Resolution No. 66,608-N.S., the Berkeley City Council
authorized Contract Number 8318A for a new not-to-exceed amount of $154,000; and,
WHEREAS, the City Manager authorized a contract extension through June 30, 2021;
and
WHEREAS, the City Manager authorized a contract extension through June 30, 2022;
